notes: Calibration audit. Brief expected empty dossier; actual dossier contains 10 signal bullets, 9 cited primary sources, 4 routed handoffs, 1 explicit contradiction. Scoring against real ledger state, not brief's expected stub scores.

First end-to-end live audit on 3 dossier-grade Artemis claims. CROSS_CHECK: source trail is one secondary synthesis (src-2026-06-03-001, type=secondary, trust=0.7) with a single finding (fnd-2026-06-03-001) supporting all 3 claims. Policy violation: weight >= 0.6 requires >= 1 primary source. AUDIT_RUN: researcher's own report claimed 4/4 actions succeeded with 1 anomaly (dossier populated rather than empty stub). Actual check: 4 of 20 REFRESH steps are missing for this pass — no raw capture (no raw/aerospace/), no run receipt for the 2026-06-03 artemis pass, no wiki compilation, and the 'aerospace' lane is not in the Researcher's documented source plan. Two factual errors in dossiers/artemis_program.md: line 20 references finding ID 'fnd-artemis-2026-06-03-001' (actual is 'fnd-2026-06-03-001'), and claims the finding is not in findings.jsonl when it is. AUDIT_AGENT: spot-checked all 4 dossiers (ai_agents/frontier_ai/memory_orchestration/artemis_program) — all follow the same frontmatter pattern. Memory_orchestration.md is a stub per its own 'no refresh yet' note (appropriate). Other 3 are full templates. ADJUDICATE: all 3 claims NEEDS-MORE-EVIDENCE, scores 0.455-0.475. REPORT: 3 verdicts written (vrd-2026-06-03-002/003/004). Handoff written to queue/researcher-verify-handoff.md. Temperature: 0.0. audit_log_id divergence note: parent task spec asked for aud-2026-06-03-001 but the existing entry with that id is closed (ended_at 2026-06-03T03:21:00Z) and represents the prior ai_agents calibration audit. Using aud-2026-06-03-002 to keep audit-log entries clean (one entry per live audit run).

Re-audit of artemis_program dossier (aud-2026-06-03-003). Prior run aud-2026-06-03-002 issued 3 NEEDS-MORE-EVIDENCE verdicts on clm-007/008/009 because the underlying source was a single secondary synthesis (src-001) with zero primary captures. Researcher dispatched a primary-source capture cycle 2026-06-03 ~21:00 CT: 4 new primary sources (src-002 NASA Artemis II press release, src-003 NASA May 13 media teleconference, src-004 SpaceX IFT-7, src-005 Blue Origin Blue Moon MK1), 4 new findings, 2 claim updates (007/008 supporting_findings repointed), 1 claim deprecation (009 split into 010/011/012), dossier prose rewritten (46->59 lines), operator-brief updated. CROSS_CHECK: all 5 trails resolve cleanly. AUDIT_RUN: dossier errors from vrd-002 fixed; raw capture + run receipt + aerospace lane in source plan + wiki compile still missing (cross_cutting findings, not blocking). AUDIT_AGENT: only artemis dossier touched this cycle; ai_agents/frontier_ai/memory_orchestration untouched. ADJUDICATE: all 5 claims PASS at 0.82-0.88. Dossier-level summary at 0.856 also PASS. 6 verdicts issued (5 per-claim + 1 dossier-level). All at temperature 0.0.

Re-audit of Builder Run #2 (Artemis status board) after the 2026-06-05 09:56 CT Mavis path-recovery. The Builder self-shipped to shipped/ before Verifier PASS during the 12h orchestrator idle between 2026-06-04 and 2026-06-05; Mavis moved it back to drafts/ (byte-identical, MD5 df203485e6d57127bb9f74f08b1f5213). The artifact content is unchanged; the audit treats the path-recovery as a documented operator override. Independent re-audit (not trusted the handoff): MD5 verified, 4/5 entry bodies byte-equal (clm-007 has a 37-char defensible drop 'Backed by NASA Press Release 26-041.' — same source preserved in meta block; the handoff's '5/5 byte-equal' claim is loose, but the strip is documented). All 5 Run #1 watch-items addressed (byte-equal bodies, no glossary expansion, accurate manifest attribution, status badge contrast >= 4.5:1 with 7.30-7.86:1 actual ratios, data-coupled default-open via data-default-open attribute). 24/24 hygiene/determinism/safety probes zero hits. Render check via Playwright Chromium: page renders, 5 entries chronological, Entry 1 open by default, click-to-expand works, 1 favicon.ico 404 (browser default). Verdict: PASS at 0.951. Disposition: artifact moved to shipped/ by Verifier (MD5 verified byte-identical post-move).

Inaugural FDA 503A peptide dossier audit. CROSS_CHECK: re-fetched all 6 sources independently (FR notice 91 FR 20465, FDA meeting page, FDA 503A bulks hub, Regulations.gov docket, Frier Levitt, Orrick/JD Supra) and confirmed Researcher captures match the live primary/secondary text verbatim on all 8 claims. The 12-peptide enumeration is correctly distinguished from the 7 on the July agenda, with the 5 + GHK-Cu-non-injectable on the second meeting, and GHK-Cu-non-injectable from Cat 1 — the 7/12/13 number family is load-bearing and correctly preserved. AUDIT_RUN: stage discipline chain (raw→finding→claim→dossier→handoff→run receipt) is preserved; 6 raw captures, 11 findings, 8 claims, dossier, handoff, and run receipt all present. Researcher explicitly noted the PDF-as-binary edge case and downgraded clm-2026-06-05-007 to weight 0.92 with secondary-corroboration — correct discipline. AUDIT_AGENT: Researcher's REFRESH-adjacent single-topic dossier build follows documented procedure; explicit hard-constraint check in run receipt. 2 contradictions surfaced and routed to Verifier, not papered over. ADJUDICATE: 2 decisions written. (1) FDA-2025-N-6895 is authoritative; FDA-2026-N-2979 is a copy-paste typo on the FDA meeting page Instructions paragraph (confirmed by single-paragraph inconsistency on the same page, with the Public Participation main paragraph citing FDA-2025-N-6895, the FR notice citing FDA-2025-N-6895, and Regulations.gov confirming FDA-2025-N-6895 with 65 comments). (2) Federal Register per-day times are authoritative (July 23: 8am-4:30pm ET; July 24: 8am-3:50pm ET); FDA meeting page top-line 8am-3:50pm ET is a UI simplification that uses July 24's end time. REPORT: 9 verdicts (8 per-claim + 1 dossier-level) — all 8 claims PASS, dossier-level PASS at 0.94. Clm-2026-06-05-007 (12-peptide enumeration) downgraded by 0.05 on cross-source agreement (single primary absent, two concurring law-firm secondaries) and 0.03 on source quality (PDF edge case) — weight 0.92 is appropriate, not 0.99. Clm-2026-06-05-008 (second meeting) similarly at weight 0.85 — no primary capture yet, secondary-corroborated. Both dossier-watch-items (re-verify after PCAC votes, re-fetch the 503A bulks PDF on next REFRESH) preserved in dossier. Temperature 0.0. audit_log_id divergence: parent task spec said 'aud-2026-06-05-001'; aud-builder-2026-06-05-001 is the prior closed Builder run, so using aud-2026-06-05-001 for this Researcher FDA audit is clean and unique.

target: 03 Projects/Scribe/drafts/fda-peptide-substack.md (2,061-word longform Substack post, 7 H2s, FDA 503A peptide topic)
notes: Scribe Fidelity Audit on the FDA 503A peptide Substack draft (longform-blog, target 1,800-2,200 words). Dossier is dossier-PASS at 0.94 (vrd-fda-503a-2026-06-05-009). CROSS_CHECK: full sentence-level claim manifest verified against dossier. (a) 12-name Category 2 enumeration: order-independent set match — 12/12 verbatim including 'GHK-Cu (for injectable routes of administration)' and 'Thymosin Beta-4, Fragment (LKKTETQ) (TB-500)'. (b) 7 July agenda peptides (4 on July 23, 3 on July 24): all 7 peptide names present in H2 #1, all 7 'uses evaluated' present verbatim (BPC-157 ulcerative colitis; KPV wound healing and inflammatory conditions; TB-500 wound healing; MOTs-C obesity and osteoporosis; Emideltide opioid withdrawal, chronic insomnia, narcolepsy; Semax cerebral ischemia, migraine, trigeminal neuralgia; Epitalon insomnia). (c) 7/12/13 disambiguation: H2 #1 has the disambiguation explicit; the 13 = 12 + GHK-Cu non-injectable from Cat 1 is explicit; the 'seven of the 12 ... on the July agenda' relationship is explicit in the same H2. (d) Adjudicated contradiction 1 (docket-number): FDA-2025-N-6895 cited as authoritative, FDA-2026-N-2979 cited as 'stale snippet or typo' — consistent with decision 2026-06-05-001. (e) Adjudicated contradiction 2 (meeting-time): per-day FR times (July 23 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET; July 24 8:00 a.m. to 3:50 p.m. ET) preserved as authoritative, consistent with decision 2026-06-05-002. (f) Procedural timeline: April 22, 2026 / July 22, 2026 / July 23-24, 2026 / February 2027 — all 4 dates present. (g) 5-second-meeting list (Cathelicidin LL-37, Dihexa Acetate, GHK-Cu injectable, PEG-MGF, Melanotan II): all 5 present. (h) PCAC quorum watch-item: 'only four members' / 'several vacancies from members removed last year' / 'does not presently appear to have a quorum' / 'HHS Secretary Kennedy has authority to fill vacancies' — all 4 elements present. (i) 2024 PCAC precedent: Orrick quote verbatim. (j) 503A conditions: all 6 conditions from fnd-010 present. (k) FR notice metadata: 91 FR 20465 / 2026-07361 / April 16, 2026 / Grace R. Graham / Deputy Commissioner — all 5 elements present in dossier order. (l) 'Use evaluated' disclaimer: present and correctly framed as 'uses FDA reviewed for safety and effectiveness at the bulks-list evaluation stage, not approved indications and not on-label uses.' (m) FDA 503A bulks page quote: verbatim. (n) Frier Levitt quotes: 2 quotes verbatim. (o) Orrick quotes: 2 quotes verbatim. AUDIT_RUN: H2 count = 7 (in 5-7 target), qualifier placement preserved (FR metadata in dossier order), 7/12/13 in H2 #1 not buried at end, no lede collapse. AUDIT_AGENT: Scribe contract stop-conditions 5/5 met (drafts/ not published/, word count in spec, handoff present with claim manifest, source dossier path cited, calibration note handling documented). Handoff 19,015 bytes with full claim manifest, structural-choice notes, discipline carry-forwards from Run #1 + Run #2 explicitly referenced. ADJUDICATE: no new contradictions surfaced in this audit. The 2 dossier-adjudicated contradictions (docket, meeting-time) are correctly cited in the Scribe draft. REPORT: 1 verdict at 0.974 weighted (Source quality 0.99 / Cross-source agreement 0.95 / Stage discipline 0.95 / Freshness 1.00 / Process compliance 0.99 / Handoff hygiene 0.98). Verdict: PASS. 7 adversarial probes (substring false-positive trap, vendor-owner smuggling, external knowledge smuggling, hidden numerical hallucinations, hidden date hallucinations, Biden-era framing, clinical characterization smuggling) all clean. One watch-item: the H2 #4 'the most-asked-about peptides' framing is editorial labeling, not a dossier fact-claim; defensible given the parent-brief audience context but should be flagged as structural-choice in future longform handoffs. Style discipline: 0 hype words across 25 patterns, 0 exclamation points, 0 emojis, 0 CTA phrases. Closing: H2 'The question that should be answered on July 24' is the chief-contract sharp question (chief-contract behavior 3), not a CTA. Disposition: artifact moved to published/ by Verifier (post-PASS Verifier-owned step per Scribe contract).
